description: A minimal Graffiticode display dialect with three primitives: `hello` for rendering a greeting string, `image` for rendering an image from a URL, and `theme "dark"/"light" (body)` for wrapping either in a light or dark visual theme. Emits one rendered element per program. For richer programs (arithmetic, lists, lambdas, pattern matching) use L0002; for domain-specific artifacts use the corresponding dialect.

example_prompts.prompt: Render a hello greeting for 'world' in dark mode.
example_prompts.notes: Canonical themed program — `theme "dark" (hello "world")..` with the body in parentheses.

example_prompts.prompt: Say hello to 'reader' with the light theme.
example_prompts.notes: Exercises the other theme name so the translator doesn't hardcode `"dark"`.

example_prompts.prompt: Display the image at https://example.com/photo.png in dark mode.
example_prompts.notes: Exercises `theme` wrapping `image` rather than `hello` — the same `theme "dark" (body)` shape applies.

example_prompts.prompt: Show the picture at https://example.com/cat.jpg with light theme.
example_prompts.notes: Light-themed image — alternate theme + alternate URL so the translator doesn't overfit.

example_prompts.prompt: Render a plain hello greeting for 'world' without a theme.
example_prompts.notes: Unthemed program — `hello "world"..` by itself; makes clear that `theme` is optional.

example_prompts.prompt: Display the image at https://example.com/photo.png without any theme wrapper.
example_prompts.notes: Unthemed image — `image "https://example.com/photo.png"..`; the no-theme variant of the image case.
